name: Code Discovered CALM Architecture
description: Initial CALM architecture scaffold populated from static code discovery output.

nodes.name: Repo Users
nodes.description: Developers, architects, and operators using the monorepo tools and user interfaces.

nodes.name: CALM Hub Platform
nodes.description: Hub-related subsystem combining web UI, backend API, MCP endpoint, storage, and optional authentication.

nodes.name: CALM Hub UI
nodes.description: React and Vite browser UI for browsing and visualizing CALM resources.

nodes.name: CALM Hub API
nodes.description: Quarkus backend serving /calm APIs and Swagger UI.

nodes.name: CALM Hub MCP Server
nodes.description: Embedded MCP endpoint exposed by CALM Hub at /mcp.

nodes.name: MongoDB Store
nodes.description: Default persistence for CALM Hub in non-standalone mode.

nodes.name: NitriteDB Store
nodes.description: Embedded standalone persistence option for CALM Hub.

nodes.name: Keycloak
nodes.description: Optional OIDC identity provider for the secure CALM Hub profile.

nodes.name: CALM Tooling
nodes.description: Local and IDE-oriented toolchain around CALM authoring and validation.

nodes.name: CALM CLI
nodes.description: Node-based CLI for generate, validate, template, docify, and AI bootstrap commands.

nodes.name: CALM Validation Server
nodes.description: Standalone HTTP validation service exposing /health and /calm/validate.

nodes.name: CALM VS Code Extension
nodes.description: IDE extension for live preview, validation, navigation, and documentation workflows.

nodes.name: CALM AI Prompt Pack
nodes.description: Prompt templates and assistant configs consumed by calm init-ai and AI-assisted workflows.

nodes.name: CALM Suite
nodes.description: Product area containing CalmStudio and CALMGuard.

nodes.name: CalmStudio
nodes.description: Svelte-based visual editor that produces CALM JSON.

nodes.name: CalmStudio MCP
nodes.description: MCP server for architecture CRUD, validation, render, and import or export.

nodes.name: CALMGuard
nodes.description: Next.js compliance analysis app with SSE streaming and multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: External LLM Providers
nodes.description: Gemini, Claude, GPT, and Grok providers used by CALMGuard.

nodes.name: GitHub
nodes.description: Remote repository and pull request platform referenced by CALMGuard and CI workflows.

relationships.description: Users explore and manage CALM models through the Hub browser UI.
Hub UI calls the Hub backend /calm endpoints.
MCP-capable clients can use the Hub MCP endpoint.
Default Hub persistence uses MongoDB.
Standalone Hub mode uses NitriteDB storage.
Hub UI can authenticate against Keycloak when OIDC is enabled.
Secure Hub profile validates authenticated access through Keycloak.
CALM Hub Platform includes the browser UI.
CALM Hub Platform includes the Quarkus API.
CALM Hub Platform includes the embedded MCP service.
Users run local CLI commands to generate, validate, and document architectures.
CLI can be pointed at a CalmHub URL for loading documents.
Users edit CALM files in VS Code with live preview and validation.
VS Code workflows consume local CALM prompt assets.
Users invoke the standalone validation API.
Validation requests are sent to the validation endpoint.
Users draw architectures visually in CalmStudio.
AI clients can drive CalmStudio through its MCP server.
CALM Suite includes CalmStudio.
CALM Suite includes the CalmStudio MCP service.
CALM Suite includes CALMGuard.
Users upload or fetch CALM models and review compliance dashboards.
CALMGuard streams agent progress and findings back to the browser.
CALMGuard calls external LLM providers for analysis and generation.
CALMGuard fetches CALM from GitHub and produces PR-oriented outputs.
